verses.text: David asks God to hide him from the secret plots of the wicked, who whet their tongues like swords and shoot bitter words at the blameless. Slander is their weapon. The hidden malice of the tongue can wound like an arrow.

David asks God to hide him from the secret plots of the wicked, who whet their tongues like swords and shoot bitter words at the blameless. Slander is their weapon. The hidden malice of the tongue can wound like an arrow.

David asks God to hide him from the secret plots of the wicked, who whet their tongues like swords and shoot bitter words at the blameless. Slander is their weapon. The hidden malice of the tongue can wound like an arrow.

David asks God to hide him from the secret plots of the wicked, who whet their tongues like swords and shoot bitter words at the blameless. Slander is their weapon. The hidden malice of the tongue can wound like an arrow.
They encourage one another in evil, lay snares, and boast that none will see, having 'searched out' their schemes. They feel secure in secrecy. But no plot is hidden from the eye of God.
They encourage one another in evil, lay snares, and boast that none will see, having 'searched out' their schemes. They feel secure in secrecy. But no plot is hidden from the eye of God.
Yet God shoots His own arrow at them, and they fall suddenly; all men fear and declare His work. Their craft recoils on them. God turns the secret plotters into a public lesson of His justice.
Yet God shoots His own arrow at them, and they fall suddenly; all men fear and declare His work. Their craft recoils on them. God turns the secret plotters into a public lesson of His justice.
Yet God shoots His own arrow at them, and they fall suddenly; all men fear and declare His work. Their craft recoils on them. God turns the secret plotters into a public lesson of His justice.
'The righteous shall be glad in the LORD, and shall trust in him,' and the upright shall glory. Deliverance ends in joy. What humbles the wicked gladdens and confirms the faith of the godly.
